specific_mechanism: Tuned mass damper (TMD): a secondary mass-spring-damper attached to a primary structure tuned to the structure's resonant frequency; the TMD absorbs vibration energy via destructive interference between primary and damper motion, broadening the effective damping spectrum
llm_application: Attach a 'tuned damper' sidecar module whose internal resonant frequency matches a specific LLM failure mode (e.g., refusal oscillation, repetition loop, hallucination cascade); the damper absorbs amplification through destructive interference with the base model output before the failure mode resonates. Each known failure mode gets one TMD; the set is tunable post hoc.
novelty_claim: Existing safety/repair work treats failure modes with output-level filters or fine-tuning. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a parallel sidecar module tuned to a target failure-mode frequency that absorbs amplification via destructive interference.
